response: Alex has been exploring new ideas through reading, which has expanded their understanding of love and relationships. This shift in perspective has been both enlightening and challenging. The first novel Alex read was 120 pages long, which took them 4 days to finish. This novel introduced new concepts that sparked deep thought. The second novel was longer, with 180 pages, and it took Alex 6 days to complete. This book offered a different viewpoint on how love can be expressed. The third novel was even more extensive, with 240 pages, and it required 8 days to read. This final book provided the most detailed exploration of relationships. Alex read each novel with a consistent pace, which made the process more manageable. The consistency in reading speed helped Alex maintain focus throughout the books. By reading the same number of pages each day, Alex ensured a steady progress. This method allowed Alex to absorb the content without feeling overwhelmed. The first novel required 30 pages per day, which was a manageable number. The second novel needed 30 pages per day as well, which matched the first book's rate. The third novel demanded 30 pages per day, keeping the pace uniform across all three. This uniformity made it easier for Alex to track their progress. Each novel was read at the same daily rate, which made the reading experience more structured. The same number of pages per day helped Alex stay committed to their reading goal. Dividing the total pages by the total days gives the average pages per day. The average pages per day across all three novels was 30.
